zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabel de Clare於1161年出生在英國素福爾可克萊爾時，她的父親Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford45歲，母親Matilda de Saint-Hilaire29歲。她於大約1192年與Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1203年在英國諾圖波藍德波塔爾享年42歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾白藍德 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabel de Clare于1161年出生在英国素福爾可克萊爾时，她的父亲Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford45岁，母亲Matilda de Saint-Hilaire29岁。她于大约1192年与Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1203年在英国諾圖波藍德波塔爾享年42岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾白藍德 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabel de Clare was born in 1161, in Clare, Suffolk, England, her father, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, was 45 and her mother, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, was 29. She married Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal about 1192. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1203, in Bothal, Northumberland, England, at the age of 42, and was buried in Byland Abbey, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mabel de Clare est née en 1161, à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal environ 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1203, à Bothal, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Byland Abbey, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Byland Abbey: Byland Abbey, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Mabel de Clare 1161 in Clare, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford 45 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Saint-Hilaire 29. Sie hat um 1192 Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1203 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Bothal, Northumberland, England und wurde in Byland Abbey, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal circa 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1203, nel Bothal, Northumberland, England, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolta a Byland Abbey, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clareが1161年にイギリススフォルククラーで生まれた時，父Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertfordは45歳，母Matilda de Saint-Hilaireは29歳であった。約1192年にRobert Bertram Baron of Bothalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1203年にイギリスノースウンバーランドボサルで42歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアブイランド アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare(이)가 1161년 영국 수폴크 클래르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Saint-Hilaire(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1192년 Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1203년 영국 노르스벌랜드 벗할에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 빌랜드 애브브이에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal aproximadamente 1192. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabel de Clare родилась в 1161 г. в местности клар, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal около 1192 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1203 г. в местности ботэл, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности биланд эбби, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
